steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence “This bone is called the hyoid.” in the context of the whole passage. Read what comes before and after: before, the passage mentions a U-shaped bone; after, every sentence uses the word “hyoid” to compare jungle cats and leopards. The sentence’s job is structural.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The sentence introduces a term that is used in the discussion that follows.’: Correct. The sentence names the bone “hyoid,” and every subsequent sentence uses that term to drive the comparison between jungle cats and leopards. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘The sentence explains the unique nature of the researchers’ hypothesis.’: The sentence does not explain anything about the hypothesis—it only names a bone. It contains no information about what the researchers found or why the hypothesis is unique. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The sentence summarizes the debate presented in the text.’: There is no debate in the passage. The text presents one hypothesis, not opposing views. A bare naming sentence cannot summarize a debate. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The sentence identifies the location of a bone in felines.’: The sentence says nothing about where the hyoid is located in the body. The previous sentence says it is in the throat, not this one. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
